# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

APP_NAME="myapp"
TMP_BASE="/tmp/${APP_NAME}-$$"
TMP_IN="${TMP_BASE}.tmp"
TMP_OUT="${TMP_BASE}.out"
TMP_LOG="${TMP_BASE}.log"

cleanup() {
  rm -f -- "$TMP_IN" "$TMP_OUT" "$TMP_LOG"
}
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM HUP

umask 077

: >"$TMP_LOG"

write_input() {
  cat >"$TMP_IN"
}

process_data() {
  : >"$TMP_OUT"
  while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
    printf '%s\n' "$line" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]'
  done <"$TMP_IN" >>"$TMP_OUT"
}

read_results() {
  cat "$TMP_OUT"
}

write_input
process_data
read_results
